$comment: Improvement-sharing inbound fixtures, case (d): INBOUND human-only-target contribution. release-spec roadmap #4 / DD-7(4); DD-6(1) human-only boundary; mutability.js. Shaped as evaluate.js expects (top-level kind:'rule-diff' + target + change), carrying NO instance/brand specifics — but its TARGET is a guardrail/safety rule (frontmatter mutability:human-only, category:safety). The maintainer harness `evaluate` MUST REJECT it via engine/self-improve/mutability.js assertMachineChangeAllowed (EHUMANONLY): a contribution that would modify a human-only safety surface is never assimilable by the loop, regardless of its evidence or its (here deliberately TIGHTENING) direction. Making the change a TIGHTENING (soft->hard, warn->block) proves the rejection comes from the human-only boundary (EHUMANONLY), NOT from never-loosen — the two structural refusals are independent. The harness checks the human-only boundary FIRST (most-restrictive wins), so the expected code is EHUMANONLY. Synthetic. See expected/evaluate-outcomes.json.
summary: Strengthen the claims-safety guardrail (tighten a safety rule).
rationale: Well-intentioned: proposes making a safety rule STRICTER. Still inadmissible to the automated loop — safety rules are human-only.
